title: Policy Memo Writer Agent
problem: Government policy teams spend days manually researching prior memos, regulations, and stakeholder inputs before drafting a new policy memo.
solution: Agent researches relevant prior memos, regulations, and public comments in parallel, synthesizes key points, then drafts a formatted policy memo in the agency's house style.
whoItsFor: Policy Analysts, Legislative Staff, Government Communications Teams
workflowSteps: Policy brief or topic inputted by analyst
Agent runs parallel searches: internal memo archive, regulatory database, public comment records
Synthesizes relevant findings into structured outline
Drafts memo in agency house style with executive summary, background, recommendations
Sends to supervisor for review; annotates sources for every claim
keyInsights: Source attribution is non-negotiable in government. Every claim in the memo must trace back to a retrievable document — this is the HITL + audit trail pattern applied to written output rather than a database transaction.

relatedExams.why: Prompt and Context Engineering — structured output, house style

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart TD
  A([Policy Brief Input]) --> B[Parallel Research]
  B --> C1[Internal Memo Archive]
  B --> C2[Regulatory Database]
  B --> C3[Public Comment Records]
  C1 & C2 & C3 --> D[Synthesize Key Findings]
  D --> E[Draft Memo with Source Attribution]
  E --> F([Supervisor Review])
architectureNotes: The agent receives a legislative text URL and optional focus topics. It runs a multi-pass summarisation: first a structural pass to extract sections, then a policy-impact pass per section, then a cross-section synthesis. The final memo follows a fixed template (summary, key changes, stakeholder impact, recommended actions). A reviewer step checks for unsupported claims before the memo is sent.

failureModes.mode: LLM fabricates a statutory reference not in the source
failureModes.mitigation: Every factual claim must link to a direct quote from the parsed document; citation check step compares each claim against retrieved text before memo is released.

failureModes.mode: Legislation website changes structure, breaking parser
failureModes.mitigation: Use multiple fallback parsers (Trafilatura → BeautifulSoup → PDF download); alert on parse failures; human review gate if primary parser fails.

failureModes.mode: Multi-section synthesis loses nuance of exceptions and carve-outs
failureModes.mitigation: Preserve section-level summaries as structured data before synthesis; exceptions/carve-outs extracted with dedicated prompt pass.

scalingConsiderations: Typical memo generation: 3–5 LLM calls (structural pass, section summaries, cross-section synthesis, compliance check, formatting); total ~30–60s at sonnet pricing.
For high-volume policy teams (50+ memos/month): pre-cache parsed document structure; only re-run synthesis when a new amendment is detected.
Multi-jurisdiction support: parameterise by jurisdiction (US federal, state, EU, UK); maintain jurisdiction-specific templates and citation styles.

integrations.note: Scrape or download bill text and regulatory filings
Cross-reference with existing case law and regulations
Archives final memo with version history
Delivers memo to policy team for review
